app.title: Nabd
app.subtitle: Clinical workspace, CDS, and predictive insights
language.switcher.label: Language
welcome.title: Smarter, safer medical records
welcome.subtitle: Nabd is a FHIR-based platform that unifies patient records, real-time clinical decision support, and predictive AI insights in one secure workspace for care teams and patients.
welcome.feature.records.title: Unified clinical records
welcome.feature.records.desc: Manage patients, encounters, conditions, medications, and more on a standards-based FHIR record that stays consistent across the care team.
welcome.feature.cds.title: Clinical decision support
welcome.feature.cds.desc: Configurable rules fire real-time safety alerts as clinicians prescribe and document, helping prevent errors before they reach the patient.
welcome.feature.ai.title: Predictive AI insights
welcome.feature.ai.desc: Built-in risk models surface high-risk patients early, so teams can act on readmission and deterioration risk before it escalates.
welcome.feature.portal.title: Patient self-service portal
welcome.feature.portal.desc: Patients securely view their own health records, manage their profile, and stay on top of upcoming care from any device.

conditions.guide.whatBody: A condition records a diagnosis, problem, or health concern — for example hypertension, type 2 diabetes, or a fracture.

observations.create.heroSubtitle: Record a measurement, vital sign, or lab result for the patient.

observations.guide.title: About Observations
observations.guide.whatBody: An observation is a single measurement or finding — a vital sign, a lab result, or a clinical assessment such as blood pressure or weight.
observations.guide.statusBody: Use preliminary for results awaiting confirmation, and final once the result is verified.
observations.form.codeHint: The test, vital sign, or finding — e.g. Blood pressure, Body weight (LOINC codes work too).
observations.form.valueHint: The measured result, including units where relevant — e.g. 120/80 mmHg, 72 kg.

procedures.guide.title: About Procedures
procedures.guide.whatBody: A procedure records an action performed on a patient — a surgery, a therapy session, or a diagnostic intervention such as an appendectomy or an X-ray.
procedures.guide.statusBody: Use in-progress while the procedure is underway and completed once it is done.

allergies.guide.title: About Allergies
allergies.guide.whatTitle: What is an allergy or intolerance?
allergies.guide.whatBody: This records a substance the patient reacts to — a medication, food, or environmental trigger such as penicillin or peanuts. It helps decision support flag risky prescriptions.
allergies.guide.statusBody: Mark an allergy active while it applies, and inactive or resolved if the patient no longer reacts.
allergies.form.codeHint: The substance the patient reacts to — e.g. Penicillin, Peanut (SNOMED CT codes work too).

appointments.guide.statusBody: Use booked for a confirmed slot, arrived when the patient checks in, and fulfilled once the visit is complete.

encounters.guide.title: About Encounters
encounters.guide.whatBody: An encounter captures a single interaction between a patient and a provider — an office visit, an admission, or a telehealth call.
encounters.guide.statusBody: Use planned before the visit, in-progress while the patient is being seen, and finished once care is complete.
encounters.form.classCodeHint: How the visit takes place — e.g. AMB (outpatient), IMP (inpatient), EMER (emergency).

externalAi.panel.title: External AI Models
externalAi.panel.subtitle: Send this patient's record to a registered remote model and read back its decision.
externalAi.noActive: No active external models are registered.
externalAi.warning: Running a model sends this patient's FHIR data (demographics, observations, conditions, medications, allergies) to the selected external service. Only run models you trust.
externalAi.admin.subtitle: Register remote ("away") AI model endpoints. A practitioner can then send a patient's FHIR record to one and read back its decision. Endpoints must use HTTPS; secrets are encrypted at rest.
externalAi.admin.decisionPathHelp: Dot-path into the response to surface as the decision. Leave blank to return the whole body.
ai.title: AI Insights
ai.intro: Three machine-learning models score every patient straight from their FHIR record — type-2 diabetes, cardiovascular (heart disease), and 30-day hospital readmission risk. Each ONNX classifier is trained on a public clinical dataset and reads only the features Nabd can actually observe (age, vitals, labs, conditions, medications and visits), imputing the training median when a value is missing. Scores are decision support — illustrative, not clinically validated.
ai.modelEmpty: No assessable patients yet. Import patients with a birth date to activate this model.
ai.filterHigh: Click to show only High risk patients
ai.filterMedium: Click to show only Medium risk patients
ai.filterLow: Click to show only Low risk patients
ai.imputedNote: {0} of {1} scored with one or more imputed (estimated) features
ai.noMatch: No patients match your search or filter.
ai.howSubtitle: Shared design across all three models
ai.how.offlineBody: Each model is a scikit-learn RandomForest trained in Google Colab, exported to ONNX, and run inside the API. The model output is the score directly — there are no hand-tuned rules.
ai.how.featuresBody: Models are trained on only the columns Nabd can recover from a real FHIR record, so training matches inference. A missing value is filled with the training-set median and the patient is flagged as estimated (shown with ≈).
ai.how.cdsBody: The readmission score is also exposed to the CDS rule engine (ai.riskLevel, ai.compositeScore, …), so authored rules can fire cards based on AI risk.
ai.popSubtitle: Top conditions recorded across all patients in this workspace.
ai.popEmpty: No condition data available yet. Add conditions to patient records to see prevalence.
ai.ext.title: External AI Models
ai.ext.subtitle: Pick a patient, then send their record to a registered remote model.
ai.model.readmission.title: 30-Day Readmission Risk
ai.model.readmission.predicts: Chance of an unplanned hospital readmission within 30 days
ai.model.diabetes.title: Type-2 Diabetes Risk
ai.model.cardiovascular.title: Cardiovascular Risk
